title: Synthex Stress Corpus Manifest
description: Versioned, hashed manifest for the epic stress-test corpus (V2_PLAN P4.1). The corpus is a fixed, reproducible set of >=1000 artifacts split into a benign subset (real public pages, incl. OWASP/security content for the describing-vs-executing test) and a LABELED-adversarial subset (OWASP/ATLAS-mapped injection packs + a 'Bag of Tricks' format-manipulation set, arXiv:2510.11570). Each artifact carries a content sha256 so the run is deterministic and independently auditable. The harness (run.mjs) reads this manifest to load the corpus; NO artifact bytes live in the manifest itself.

manifest_version.description: Schema/manifest version. Append-only evolution: new fields are additive and optional; bump only on an incompatible root-key change.

corpus_id.description: Stable identifier for this exact corpus revision (e.g. 'synthex-stress-2026-06-01'). Changing the corpus content REQUIRES a new corpus_id so prior results.json stay attributable.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the manifest was generated.

frameworks.description: Threat/standard taxonomies the adversarial subset is mapped against. Use ONLY canonical forms (V2_PLAN P2.7): OWASP LLM 2025 ids, OWASP Agentic 2026 (ASI01-ASI10), MITRE ATLAS v5.6.0 (AML.T0051/.001), Bag-of-Tricks arXiv:2510.11570. Do not invent ids.

totals.description: Aggregate counts. 'artifacts' MUST be the length of artifacts[] and MUST be >= 1000 (the P4.1 floor; 10000 is the stretch). benign + adversarial MUST equal artifacts.

subsets.description: Per-subset metadata. The benign subset is the FP axis; the adversarial subset is the recall axis (V2_PLAN P4.1 dimension 3).

artifacts.description: The corpus index. Each entry is one stress artifact with a content hash so the run is deterministic. >= 1000 entries.

id.description: Stable per-artifact id, unique within the corpus.

source.description: Where the artifact came from: a URL for benign real pages, or a fixture path for constructed adversarial samples.

surface.description: Bright Data surface the benign artifact maps to. ONLY the probe-confirmed real surfaces. 'fixture' for constructed adversarial samples that never hit BD.

subtype.description: Sub-label driving the describing-vs-executing FP test. 'executing' = content commands the reading agent (true positive target). 'describing'/'neutral' = benign, MUST NOT be flagged or the FP rate is wrong. 'review' = deliberately borderline. Mirrors test/fixtures/guard-recall-corpus subtypes.

expected_verdict.description: Ground-truth label for the recall/FP scoring. BLOCK for executing injections, ALLOW for benign, REVIEW for borderline.

framework.description: Canonical taxonomy id for adversarial artifacts (e.g. 'OWASP LLM01', 'AML.T0051', 'ASI01'). Omit or empty for benign.

technique.description: Short adversarial technique name (adapted from published patterns only; no novel zero-days).

content_sha256.description: Lowercase hex SHA-256 of the artifact content bytes. Makes the corpus content-addressed and the run deterministic.
